EnteteFenetre: EncapsArduino v.2 : Creating or modifying an Arduino v2 program

from the folder :
Infotext:
This application allows you to create an encapsulated Arduino program. This means that the boards
and libraries used will remain the same throughout the maintenance of this program, provided they
have not been updated.

The application directory shown is the last one used. To change it, simply click the 'Application
Directory' button and choose a new one.
If it's a new program, the folder and the '.ino' program are automatically created, and control is
automatically transferred to the Arduino IDE.

If a new version of the board is chosen, the directory for this version is automatically created,
but it will be necessary to install this board as soon as the Arduino IDE is launched, after possibly
unlocking access to the boards and libraries.

If it's a program modification (the program is then selected from the list of programs), the file
explorer opens in the program's folder to edit the version you want to modify. There can, in fact,
be several versions of a program in the application's 'Docs' folder.
The 'Docs' folder in the program's directory also contains all the necessary documents (diagrams,
screenshots, user documentation, etc.).

The paths to the libraries and maps used by Encapsarduino are stored in a Fiparam.json file
created the first time the application is used. This file can be deleted, if desired, using the
'Reset param' function in the menu.

A copy of the Arduino IDE settings file (arduino-cli.yaml) is kept in the program folder in order to
always restore the same IDE environment for this program.

To maintain the integrity and proper functioning of encapsulated programs, it is mandatory to
use 'EncapArduino' to modify and upload them.
Aproptext:
EncapsArduino v.2.1
  Windows and Linux compatible
  Developing was done under Python3+Tkinter
  and compliled with Docker
  for backward compatibility.

  Author : Yves Le Chevalier
